mappings.rationaleAI: Disease patterns and health hazards cover characteristics and causes (A) and detection methods (B). Heart attack and myocardial infarction address cardiovascular diseases (F.1.a). Contagious disease patterns connect to infectious diseases (D.5).
History of disease understanding (A, B), treatment approaches including surgery (C.2.a), and therapeutics development (C).
Pathogen origins and transmission (A, D.5). Influenza and MRSA address infectious respiratory diseases (F.3.a).
Viral biology addresses infectious diseases (D.5). Coronaviruses connect to infectious respiratory diseases (F.3.a).
Bacteria including tuberculosis address infectious diseases (D.5) and infectious respiratory diseases (F.3.a).
Fungi as disease agents connect to infectious diseases (D.5).
Microorganisms as infection agents connect to infectious diseases (D.5).
Pandemic diseases cover disease causes (A) and infectious diseases (D.5). Tuberculosis and influenza connect to infectious respiratory diseases (F.3.a).
Plague covers infectious diseases (D.5).
HIV/AIDS covers infectious diseases (D.5) and sexually transmitted diseases (F.6.b).
Cancer addresses tumours (E), chemotherapy (C.2.d), and radiation therapy (C.2.f).

Vaccine development connects to biological therapy (C.2.b). Immune defense connects to infectious diseases (D.5). Allergies connect to allergenic diseases (D.11). Lymphoid system connects to lymphatic system (F.2).
Drug mechanisms connect to pharmacodynamic therapy (C.2.c) and chemotherapy (C.2.d). Substance toxicity connects to poisoning (D.10).
Blood composition connects to heart (F.1.a), blood vessels (F.1.b), circulation (F.1.c), and blood-forming tissues (F.1.d).
Endocrine physiology covers pituitary (F.5.a), thyroid (F.5.b), adrenals (F.5.c), and pancreas (F.5.f). Hormonal dysfunction connects to metabolic diseases (D.2).
Brain neuroanatomy covers nervous system (F.9) and central nervous system disorders (F.9.d).
Eye structure and color blindness connect to eye and vision disorders (F.9.f).
Anaesthetic practice connects to surgical treatment (C.2.a) and pharmacodynamic therapy (C.2.c).
Nutritional deficiencies address nutritional diseases (D.3). Metabolic processes connect to metabolic diseases (D.2).
Therapeutic potential connects to biological therapy (C.2.b) and organ transplants (C.2.k).
Mental disorders connect to CNS disorders (F.9.d). Psychoanalytic approaches connect to psychological therapy (C.2.l).
Schizophrenia connects to CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Depression connects to CNS disorders (F.9.d). Antidepressants connect to pharmacodynamic therapy (C.2.c).
Anxiety disorders connect to CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Neurodegenerative conditions connect to CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Autism connects to diseases present at birth (D.6) and CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Dyslexia connects to CNS disorders (F.9.d) and speech disorders (F.9.h.i).
Substance addiction connects to poisoning (D.10) and CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Psychotherapy directly addresses psychological therapy (C.2.l).
CBT directly addresses psychological therapy (C.2.l).
Medical ethics connects to therapeutic regimen factors (C.1).
Medical law connects to treatment aspects (C.1) and organ transplants (C.2.k).
Radioactivity connects to radiation therapy (C.2.f) and radiation disorders (D.8).
Microbial communities connect to infectious diseases (D.5) and digestion disorders (F.4.g).
Cognitive aging connects to CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Skeletal system connects to bone (F.8.a) and connective tissue (F.8.b).
Physiology provides disease context (A). Homeostasis connects to fluid balance (D.4).
Diet connects to nutritional diseases (D.3). Food poisoning connects to poisoning (D.10).

mappings.vsiAuthor: Steven W. Lockley, Russell G. Foster
mappings.rationaleAI: Sleep disorders connect to CNS disorders (F.9.d).
Clinical psychology connects to psychological therapy (C.2.l).
Genomics connects to disease diagnosis (B) and diseases at birth (D.6).
Developmental psychology provides context for childhood diseases (D.7).
